title: Zombie processes when container exits (pinp)
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman system service --time=0 tcp://localhost:2376` inside a rootless pod with a docker cli connecting and running a container causes the ran container to become a zombie process.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run the following script to get a shell within a container which has docker-cli installed
```shell
#!/bin/bash
podman pod create docker

podman container run --pod docker \
	--name dind --detach --privileged \
	quay.io/containers/podman:latest podman system service --log-level=debug --time=0 tcp://localhost:2376

podman container run --pod docker \
	-e "DOCKER_HOST=tcp://localhost:2376" \
	--rm --tty --interactive \
	docker.io/docker:24.0-cli

podman pod rm -f docker
```  
2. Run `docker run alpine cat /etc/os-release` within the open shell.
3. Inspect the process tree in `htop` to see zombie processes under the `podman system service` command 


### Describe the results you received

The following process the is the result of running the `docker run` command four times.
![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/16615577/96dac5c4-b06f-4cc3-906e-66a59f10931b)

### Describe the results you expected

There should be no Zombie processes when the container exits.

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I'm trying to use podman as a replacement for dockerd in a CI environment ([forgejo-runner](https://code.forgejo.org/forgejo/runner/src/branch/main/examples/kubernetes/dind-docker.yaml) / act)

comments.body:
Podman is not designed to be run as init (pid 1) process. We double fork and rely on the init to reap the childs.
As a simple fix I suggest you create the container that runs the podman service with the `--init` option.
Thank you for the quick answer, but how would I define this in the context of a pod file. Because I actually use this in the context of `podman play kube`?

Not sure if this is exposed there, @umohnani8 any idea if there is a yaml option for `--init`?

One way to work around is to just run `/usr/libexec/podman/catatonit -- podman system service ...` as command. catatonit is the binary we use as init and should be already installed in the podman image so I think this might be easy enough to do. If it is not part of the image you would need to mount it into the container.

People have been asking for one since 2019.
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/84210

Should we allow one for podman kube play --init ?
This could pass the init flag to all containers.
